data.description:
## Why

The module-size policy (`docs/architecture.md:145-186`) exists so the over-cap set
is "tracked … visible, not silently growing." That intent is currently violated:
**8** files are over the 800-LOC soft cap but the documented offenders table lists
only **4**, and the one accurate check (the CI size report) is a warn-only `echo`
that never fails and runs only on Linux. So the inventory nobody is forced to read
is the only correct one, and the human-facing table has drifted.

## What

(1) Refresh the offenders table in `docs/architecture.md` to match reality
(8 files, correct LOC, correct remedies), and (2) give the CI size report teeth so
*new* over-cap files (or significant growth of existing ones) fail the build,
while grandfathering the existing set.

## Scope

- In scope: `docs/architecture.md` (the offenders table + the 500–800 watch-band
  prose), `.github/workflows/test.yml` (the module-size report step).
- Out of scope: actually splitting any over-cap file (that is separate,
  per-file work — the soft cap is deliberately soft; do not split preemptively).

## Context (citations)

- **Actual over-cap set (verified `wc -l`, 2026-06-18):** `reconcile.py` 1308,
  `outbound_differ.py` 1277, `_cli/__init__.py` **1100 (undocumented)**,
  `__init__.py` 1036, `reads.py` 930, `config.py` **909 (undocumented)**,
  `mcp_server.py` 848, `applier.py` 801.
- **The stale table:** `docs/architecture.md:166-171` documents only 4
  (`reconcile.py`, `outbound_differ.py`, `__init__.py`, `reads.py`).
  `outbound_differ.py` is listed at `~1114` (actual 1277, +163 drift);
  `__init__.py` at `~1019` (actual 1036).
- **Active mislisting:** `docs/architecture.md:180-186` places `mcp_server.py`
  (848) and `applier.py` (801) in the "500–800 band … at the ceiling, not over
  it" — both are *over* the cap.
- **The toothless CI step:** `.github/workflows/test.yml:109-120` (step name at
  ≈112) — an `echo`/`find`/`awk '$1 > 800'` whose terminating
  `if [ -n "$over" ]; …; fi` always exits 0, gated `if: runner.os == 'Linux'`. The policy claims this is how
  "new offenders surface in PRs" (`architecture.md:164`) — but a buried,
  non-failing, Linux-only echo is weak surfacing.
- **Metric:** the report and the table both use raw `wc -l`; keep that metric.

## Implementation plan

1. **Refresh the table** (`architecture.md:166-171`): list all 8 over-cap files
   with current LOC and a one-line remedy each (the two undocumented ones:
   `_cli/__init__.py` — split the argv-routing/`_dispatch` arms from the lazy
   command-delegation seam; `config.py` — split the dataclass/schema from the
   env/CLI-override + cache machinery along the existing seam). Move
   `mcp_server.py` and `applier.py` out of the watch-band prose (180-185) into the
   table.
2. **Add a checked-in baseline allowlist** for the size gate — e.g.
   `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` listing the 8 grandfathered paths (one per
   line), with a comment that it must match the architecture.md table.
3. **Make the CI step gate on regressions** (`test.yml`): rewrite the step so it
   (a) computes the set of files with `wc -l > 800`, (b) subtracts the allowlist,
   and (c) **exits non-zero** if any file is over-cap and not allow-listed (a new
   offender). Optionally also fail if an allow-listed file grows by more than a
   tolerance (e.g. >10% over its recorded baseline) — start with the simpler
   "new-file-only" gate to avoid churn.
4. Remove the `if: runner.os == 'Linux'` restriction (the check is pure
   `wc`/shell, portable) OR keep it but document why; preferred: make it run on
   the primary lane unconditionally.
5. Add a short contributor note (in `architecture.md` near the table) that the
   allowlist and the table must be updated together, and that adding to the
   allowlist requires a remedy line in the table.
6. Test: add a throwaway 900-LOC file locally, run the gate logic, confirm it
   fails; remove it; confirm green with the real tree.

## Tradeoffs

- **Benefit:** "not silently growing" becomes self-enforcing; the table and the
  automated inventory can no longer disagree (CI fails if they do).
- **Cost/risk:** a hard gate on a deliberately *soft* cap could block a
  legitimately-cohesive large module. Mitigation: gate only on **new** over-cap
  files (allowlist grandfathers the existing 8), so existing work is unaffected and
  authors of a genuinely-needed large new unit add one allowlist line + one table
  row (a deliberate, reviewed act). The growth-tolerance check is optional — defer
  if it proves noisy.
- **Alternative considered:** keep it warn-only and just refresh the table —
  rejected; the table already drifted under exactly that regime.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/architecture.md`'s offenders table lists all 8 current over-cap files
  with accurate LOC and a remedy each; `mcp_server.py` and `applier.py` are no
  longer in the "under the cap" watch-band prose.
- [ ] A checked-in allowlist enumerates the grandfathered over-cap files.
- [ ] The CI size step exits non-zero when a non-allow-listed file exceeds 800 LOC
  (verified with a temporary oversized file), and passes on the current tree.
- [ ] The size check is not silently Linux-only (runs on the primary lane).
- [ ] A contributor note explains that the allowlist and the table change together.
</content>
data.title: Refresh the module-size offenders table + give the CI size report teeth
